  • Changelog

    Module: immuno_graphical

    module-immuno-graphical.Rmd

    Purpose

    immuno_graphical produces graphical descriptions of COVID-19 vaccine immunogenicity. For each trial it generates violin plots, two-phase sampling scatter plots, and (for most trials) demographic-stratified immunogenicity figures.

    Source module: correlates_reporting2/immuno_graphical/
    Key report: assembled into a bookdown PDF chapter via build_chapter_report(), which calls _build_chapter.sh immuno_graphical from the project root.

    Example data

    The mock datasets used by this module are located in data_clean/ under report_root:

    File Trial label Rows
    moderna_mock_data_processed_with_riskscore.csv moderna_mock ~26 k
    janssen_pooled_mock_data_processed_with_riskscore.csv janssen_pooled_mock ~38 k

    Locate them at runtime with:

    report_root  <- "/path/to/correlates_reporting2"
moderna_path <- file.path(
  report_root, "data_clean",
  "moderna_mock_data_processed_with_riskscore.csv"
)

    Reproduce the report

    1. Configure the session 

    library(utilrep2)

report_root <- "/path/to/correlates_reporting2"
setup_session(
  trial       = "moderna_mock",
  platform    = "linux",
  data_path   = file.path(report_root, "data_clean"),
  report_root = report_root
)

    2. Run the module 

    run_immuno_graphical(trial = "moderna_mock")

    This executes, in order:

    1. descriptive_graphics_data_preprocess.R — cleans and reshapes the input data into long format for downstream plotting.
    2. make_assay_lim.R — computes assay-level axis limits.
    3. descriptive_graphics_two_phase_plots.R — violin/scatter plots grouped by treatment arm and baseline serostatus.
    4. descriptive_graphics_demographic_strata.R — the same plots stratified by age, sex, race, and other demographic variables (standard trials only).

    Set clean = FALSE to skip deleting previous output:

    run_immuno_graphical(trial = "moderna_mock", clean = FALSE)

    3. Build the chapter PDF

    After the analysis completes, build the bookdown PDF chapter (Unix/Linux only):

    build_chapter_report("immuno_graphical", trial = "moderna_mock")

    Output

    Figures are saved under immuno_graphical/output/<trial>/ and immuno_graphical/output/<trial>/demographics/. The chapter PDF is written to immuno_graphical/ as covpn_correlates_immuno_graphical_<trial>_<date>.pdf.

    Report sections (from report.Rmd)

    • Set 1 – violin and paired scatter plots
    • Set 2 – two-phase sampling scatter plots
    • Set 3 – box plots by baseline serostatus
    • Demographics – stratified figures by age, sex, race/ethnicity

    Supported trials

    Trial Notes
    moderna_mock, moderna_real Full set including demographics
    vat08_combined Two-phase plots only (omicron assays)
    nextgen_mock VaxArt; extended violin/pair plots
    janssen_pooled_mock / janssen_na_mock / janssen_la_mock / janssen_sa_mock Full set
    janssen_pooled_partA Violin/scatter only
    prevent19_stage2 Two-phase plots
    All other standard trials Default branch (all four scripts)

    Developed by Y H.

    Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
